name: Desert Mountain
description: Desert Mountain is the crown jewel of North Scottsdale luxury, spanning 8,300 acres with six Jack Nicklaus-designed golf courses. This exclusive community offers unparalleled amenities, stunning high-desert terrain, and homes ranging from villas to $20M+ estates.

narrative.lead: Desert Mountain is not merely a golf community — it is a masterfully curated dialogue between world-class design and the ancient Sonoran landscape. Spanning 8,300 pristine acres in the high Sonoran Desert at elevations ranging from 2,500 to 4,500 feet, this legendary enclave represents the absolute pinnacle of Arizona luxury living. The air runs noticeably cooler than the Valley floor, the views stretch endlessly across the Valley of the Sun, and the lifestyle is nothing short of extraordinary. With six Jack Nicklaus Signature golf courses, seven distinctive clubhouses, and an amenity collection that rivals any five-star resort, Desert Mountain has established itself as one of the most significant residential developments in American golf history.

sections.content:
Founded in 1986 by visionary developer Lyle Anderson, Desert Mountain was conceived as the ultimate expression of desert golf living — a place where the game's greatest architect would have an entire mountain range as his canvas. The result is an unprecedented collection of six Jack Nicklaus Signature golf courses: Renegade, Cochise, Geronimo, Apache, Chiricahua, and Outlaw. Each course was masterfully carved into the rugged terrain with a distinct personality that reflects the dramatic landscape. The Renegade course, which opened in 1987, quickly established itself as one of the most challenging and scenic courses in the Southwest, with seven tee box positions per hole that Golf Magazine called 'the most versatile course in the world.' Cochise earned Sports Illustrated's 2024 designation as 'Best Modern Private Course Restoration' following a meticulous redesign by Chad Goetz. Geronimo climbs to the community's highest elevations with dramatic ravines and panoramic plateaus. Apache channels Scottish links tradition with rolling fairways and deceptively sloping greens. Chiricahua threads through desert canyons with sweeping Valley vistas. And Outlaw, rolling through high desert scenery, rounds out the collection. Together, these courses offer more Nicklaus-designed holes than any other community on Earth.

Desert Mountain holds a singular place in golf history as the birthplace of the legendary Skins Game, which debuted here and brought Arnold Palmer, Jack Nicklaus, Gary Player, and Lee Trevino together to compete amid spectacular desert scenery. That heritage of championship golf endures today through the club's active tournament calendar and the countless memorable rounds played across 108 holes. The Desert Mountain Club has earned Platinum Club of America status — the only club in Arizona ranked in the nation's top 20 — along with Robb Report's 'Best Golf Community in the World' designation and Distinguished Club recognition from Boardroom Magazine. The community will host the 2026 U.S. Amateur Four-Ball Championship, further cementing its place in the game's history. The master plan, designed by Taliesin Associated Architects carrying forward Frank Lloyd Wright's desert philosophy, ensures that every fairway, homesite, and trail corridor respects the natural contours of the Continental Mountains.

A typical morning at Desert Mountain might begin with sunrise yoga overlooking the Valley of the Sun, followed by a round on Geronimo — the highest-elevation course where temperatures run five to ten degrees cooler than the city below. After golf, the day unfolds through seven distinctive clubhouses, each with its own culinary identity and social atmosphere. The Apache Clubhouse delivers refined Southwestern fine dining with Valley views. Cochise offers the kind of casual camaraderie that turns neighbors into lifelong friends. And The Hideout at the recently renovated Renegade Clubhouse has become the community's most talked-about gathering spot, with craft cocktails and a menu that celebrates regional flavors. Staff members know residents by name, anticipate their preferences, and create the unmistakable warmth of a private resort that happens to be home.

Beyond the fairways, the community's amenity collection would anchor any world-class resort. The Jim Flick Golf Performance Center — a $1.6 million facility — provides tour-level instruction with video swing analysis and precision club fitting. The Sonoran Clubhouse houses a 42,000-square-foot fitness center with personal training, group classes, and an integrated physical therapy practice. The Spa at Desert Mountain offers six treatment rooms featuring therapeutic massages, acupuncture, LED light therapy, and Himalayan salt stone treatments. Nine tennis courts spanning hard, clay, and synthetic grass surfaces share a complex with eight dedicated pickleball courts, all staffed with teaching professionals running programs for every skill level.

When residents trade the manicured fairways for raw Sonoran beauty, 25 miles of private hiking and biking trails wind through 3,000 acres of preserved desert, connecting neighborhoods while offering encounters with roadrunners, mule deer, and the occasional javelina. Three resort-style swimming pools — including an adults-only heated saltwater lap pool — serve as social hubs year-round. The Youth Activities Center keeps younger residents engaged with arts programs, a Junior Golf Academy, and Junior Tennis Clinics, while Parents Night Out lets adults enjoy an evening at one of the seven clubhouse dining venues. Even healthcare is on-site, with HonorHealth operating a medical clinic within the community gates. With 2,300 members hailing from 49 states and eight countries, the social calendar runs deep: wine dinners, holiday galas, competitive leagues, themed club events, and a constant hum of activity that makes Desert Mountain feel less like a neighborhood and more like a private village with its own culture.

Desert Mountain's 8,300 acres are organized into 35 distinctive villages, each with its own character, elevation, and orientation. Residences range from beautifully appointed villas ideal for a lock-and-leave desert retreat to sprawling custom estates exceeding 15,000 square feet with every conceivable luxury. Architectural styles harmonize with the environment — natural stone, weathered copper, and floor-to-ceiling glass blur the boundary between indoors and out, letting the desert become part of the living space. Many homes feature private infinity-edge pools that dissolve into the desert horizon, outdoor kitchens designed for year-round entertaining beneath dark skies rich with stars, and detached guest casitas for extended visits from family and friends.

The community's carefully planned development ensures generous setbacks and privacy between residences while preserving the natural desert washes, ancient rock outcroppings, and centuries-old saguaro cacti that define the landscape. Homesites range from half-acre villa lots nestled among the lower elevations to expansive multi-acre estate parcels commanding panoramic views of Four Peaks, the McDowell Mountains, and city lights that sparkle to the southern horizon. Entry points start around $450,000 for premium homesites, with finished homes spanning from the high six figures to well beyond $12 million for the most exceptional custom estates.

With $800 million in real estate transactions recorded in 2023 and a $120 million five-year capital improvement plan currently underway, Desert Mountain continues to invest aggressively in its future while honoring the desert legacy that makes it irreplaceable. Only approximately 150 open lots remain in the community — a testament to nearly four decades of careful, deliberate development that has made Desert Mountain one of the most sought-after addresses in the American Southwest. For buyers seeking a legacy property in the high desert, the window is narrowing.

lifestyleFeatures.title: Smart Infrastructure

signatureAmenity.title: Six Jack Nicklaus Courses
signatureAmenity.description:
Desert Mountain's collection of six Jack Nicklaus Signature golf courses represents the largest concentration of Nicklaus-designed holes anywhere on Earth—an unprecedented achievement that has defined luxury golf community development for nearly four decades. Each course possesses its own distinct character while sharing the Golden Bear's hallmark design philosophy of strategic options, dramatic bunkering, and seamless integration with the natural landscape.

The Renegade course, which opened in 1987, established the standard with its bold elevation changes and challenging desert carries that reward accurate shot-making. Outlaw followed with a more playable design while maintaining the dramatic scenery, making it a member favorite for regular play. Geronimo's higher elevation provides cooler temperatures and some of the most spectacular views in the community, with panoramas stretching across the Valley of the Sun. Apache demands precision with its tight fairways and well-defended greens, while Cochise offers a more forgiving layout perfect for social rounds with guests. The Chiricahua course, completed in 2002, represents the culmination of Nicklaus's evolved design philosophy with its artistic bunkering and strategic shot values.

The Desert Mountain Club supports these magnificent courses with seven clubhouses, each offering its own dining experiences, social spaces, and member amenities. The Jim Flick Performance Center provides tour-level instruction and club fitting, while the practice facilities include multiple ranges, short game areas, and putting greens designed to replicate on-course conditions. With 108 holes of championship golf, multiple dining venues, spa services, fitness facilities, tennis and pickleball courts, and miles of hiking trails, Desert Mountain delivers a lifestyle that simply cannot be replicated elsewhere. The club has earned Platinum Club status and consistent recognition among America's top private clubs, a testament to its unwavering commitment to excellence in every detail.

gating: Private guard-gated, individual villages also gated

hoa.raw: $200-$500/mo (Master + Village dues, paid semi-annually; varies by village) + 0.5% Community Enhancement Fee at closing. Club dues separate ($200K initiation + ~$2,200/mo for Full Golf).

membership.raw: $200K initiation fee, ~$2,200/month dues (Full Golf). Categories: Full Golf, Seven Golf, Lifestyle. Waitlist in effect. 2,300+ members from 49 states and 8 countries.

golf.description: Renegade (7,524 yds - first course built) | Cochise (Sports Illustrated 2024 Best Modern Private Course Restoration) | Geronimo (dramatic elevation changes) | Apache (Scottish links tradition) | Outlaw (newest course) | Chiricahua (desert canyon routing) | No. 7 at Desert Mountain (par-54, 18-hole USGA-rated) | All six championship courses are Jack Nicklaus Signature designs | $1.6M Jim Flick Golf Performance Center

Elevation of ~3,000 ft provides cooler temps. $800M in real estate transactions in 2023; average home ~$2.5M, some to $20M. $120M five-year capital improvement plan underway. Only ~150 open lots remain - nearly built out. Master plan by Taliesin Associated Architects (Frank Lloyd Wright).

structuredAmenities.name: Renegade Golf Course & Clubhouse
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: First Jack Nicklaus Signature course (1987). Golf Magazine called it 'the most versatile course in the world.' 7 tee boxes, 2 pin placements per hole. Recently renovated clubhouse with The Hideout restaurant.
structuredAmenities.address: 37700 N Desert Mountain Pkwy, Scottsdale, AZ 85262

structuredAmenities.name: Cochise Golf Course
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Sports Illustrated 2024 'Best Modern Private Course Restoration.' Redesigned by Chad Goetz. Dramatic desert canyon setting.
structuredAmenities.address: 10550 E Desert Hills Dr, Scottsdale, AZ 85262

structuredAmenities.name: Geronimo Golf Course
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Dramatic elevation changes, deep ravines, broad fairways, and stunning plateaus.

structuredAmenities.name: Apache Golf Course
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Traditional approach with rolling fairways, ample greens, and deceptively sloping putting surfaces. Scottish links inspiration.

structuredAmenities.name: Chiricahua Golf Course
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Desert canyon routing with sweeping Valley vistas. Considered one of the most stunning examples of desert golf.

structuredAmenities.name: Outlaw Golf Course & Clubhouse
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Newest championship course rolling through high desert scenery. Clubhouse features the Arizona Grill.

structuredAmenities.name: No. 7 at Desert Mountain
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Par-54, 18-hole USGA-rated course. One of only two par-54 USGA-rated courses in the US. Fun, all-ages format.

structuredAmenities.name: Jim Flick Golf Performance Center
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: $1.6M facility with video swing analysis, club fitting, and in-house golf pros.
structuredAmenities.address: 37700 N Desert Mountain Pkwy, Scottsdale, AZ 85262

structuredAmenities.name: Sonoran Clubhouse & Fitness Center
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: 42,000 sq ft wellness hub. State-of-the-art gym, personal training, group fitness, physical therapy, nutrition counseling.

structuredAmenities.name: The Spa at Desert Mountain
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Six treatment rooms. Therapeutic massages, facials, acupuncture, LED therapy, Himalayan salt stone treatments.

structuredAmenities.name: Desert Mountain Tennis & Pickleball
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: 9 tennis courts (hard, clay, grass-like surfaces), 8 dedicated pickleball courts.

structuredAmenities.name: Desert Mountain Pools
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Three resort-style pools including adults-only saltwater lap pool. Year-round heated aquatic fitness.

structuredAmenities.name: Desert Mountain Trails
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: 25+ miles of private hiking and biking trails across 3,000 acres of Sonoran Desert preserve.

structuredAmenities.name: HonorHealth Medical Clinic (on-site)
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: On-site medical clinic providing health screenings, preventive medicine, and primary care.

structuredAmenities.name: Youth Activities Center (YAC)
structuredAmenities.shortDescription: Kids activity center with arts, crafts, games, Junior Golf Academy, Junior Tennis Clinics, Parents Night Out.

faqs.question: How much are HOA fees at Desert Mountain?
faqs.answer: HOA fees at Desert Mountain range from $200 to $500 per month depending on your village, covering master association and village dues paid semi-annually. There is also a 0.5% Community Enhancement Fee at closing. Club membership dues are separate, starting at approximately $200K initiation fee plus ~$2,200/month for Full Golf membership.

faqs.question: How many golf courses does Desert Mountain have?
faqs.answer: Desert Mountain features six Jack Nicklaus Signature championship golf courses: Renegade, Cochise, Geronimo, Apache, Chiricahua, and Outlaw. There is also No. 7 at Desert Mountain, a par-54 USGA-rated course. Together, these offer more Nicklaus-designed holes than any other community on Earth.

faqs.question: Is Desert Mountain guard-gated?
faqs.answer: Yes, Desert Mountain is a private guard-gated community with 24/7 staffed entry gates. Individual villages within the community also have their own gating, providing an additional layer of security and privacy.

faqs.question: What school district serves Desert Mountain?
faqs.answer: Desert Mountain is served by the Cave Creek Unified School District (CCUSD). The nearest high school is Cactus Shadows High School, ranked #1 in Maricopa County for student achievement. Nearby private school options include Notre Dame Preparatory and Scottsdale Prep (Great Hearts).

faqs.question: What is the price range for homes in Desert Mountain?
faqs.answer: Home prices at Desert Mountain range from approximately $450K for homesites to over $12M for luxury custom estates. The current median list price is approximately $3.4M, with an average price per square foot of $685. The community saw $800M in real estate transactions in 2023.

faqs.question: What amenities does Desert Mountain offer beyond golf?
faqs.answer: Beyond its six golf courses, Desert Mountain offers seven clubhouses with diverse dining options, a 42,000 sq ft fitness center, a full-service spa, three resort-style pools, 9 tennis courts, 8 pickleball courts, 25+ miles of private hiking and biking trails, the Jim Flick Golf Performance Center, a Youth Activities Center, and an on-site HonorHealth medical clinic.

faqs.question: How far is Desert Mountain from Scottsdale Airport?
faqs.answer: Desert Mountain is approximately 25-35 minutes from Scottsdale Airport (KSDL), which offers private/executive FBO services. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport (PHX) is about 40-50 minutes away with direct flights to over 100 destinations.

faqs.question: What is the elevation at Desert Mountain?
faqs.answer: Desert Mountain spans elevations from 2,500 to 4,500 feet in the high Sonoran Desert, which provides noticeably cooler temperatures than the Valley floor. The average elevation of approximately 3,000 feet means summer highs are typically 5-10 degrees lower than central Phoenix.

testimonials.quote: Desert Mountain exceeded every expectation we had. The golf is world-class, but it's the community and lifestyle that truly make it home.
testimonials.author: Michael & Sarah T.

testimonials.quote: We looked at every luxury community in Scottsdale. Nothing comes close to the combination of amenities, natural beauty, and genuine neighborhood feel.
testimonials.author: David R.

testimonials.quote: The elevation difference is real — our friends in Paradise Valley are sweltering while we're enjoying beautiful evenings on the patio.
testimonials.author: Jennifer & Mark L.
